summary: All major/minor findings from v1 resolved by coder v6. IdentityData.ResolveDefault() has try/catch, Export uses GetOrCreateDefaultAsync (consistent with Get), Data.Envelope has SensitivePropertyFilter. 1649 tests pass. No test for ResolveDefault failure path (hard to arrange without test infra), accepted. Verdict: PASS.

previous_reviews_assessed.codeanalyzer: agree — v3 findings remain valid and resolved
previous_reviews_assessed.tester: agree — v4 approval still holds, coder v6 changes don't regress tested paths

findings.issue: [RESOLVED] GetOrCreateDefaultAsync throws on save failure. ResolveDefault() now has try/catch, returns null.
findings.impact: Previously: unhandled crash on %MyIdentity% access. Now: graceful null.
findings.suggestion: Resolved. No test for failure path (accepted — hard to arrange).

findings.issue: [RESOLVED] Export now uses GetOrCreateDefaultAsync, consistent with Get.
findings.impact: Previously: Export(null) returned NotFound when identities existed but no default. Now: promotes/auto-creates like Get.
findings.suggestion: Resolved. Test updated to expect auto-create.

findings.impact: Defense-in-depth: future Compress() calls won't leak [Sensitive] properties.

findings.issue: [ACCEPTED] Rename partial failure leaves orphaned entry. Known limitation — needs transactional DataSource.
findings.impact: Very low probability. Not fixable without DataSource transactions.
findings.suggestion: Track as future improvement when DataSource gets transaction support.
